name: Fried Potatoes with Poblano Pepper Strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour vegetable oil into a deep sauté pan so that it reaches no more than one-third the height of the pan's sides.
2. Heat the oil until a deep-fat fry thermometer reads 360 degrees Fahrenheit.
3. While heating the oil, carefully char the peppers over an open flame until the skin blisters and blackens. Set aside to cool.
4. Once cooled, remove the seeds from the peppers and slice them into thin strips.
5. In a large non-stick skillet, heat 3 tablespoons of oil. Add the onion and cook until softened.
6. When the oil is heated, carefully add the potatoes in batches and cook, stirring for about 5 minutes or until they are tender, golden brown, and crispy.
7. Drain the cooked potatoes on paper towels and add them to the skillet with the onions.
8. Add the pepper strips and season with salt and pepper.
9. Top with cheese and cook for an additional minute.
10. Transfer the dish to a serving bowl and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
